summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard captures a legendary 1977 Buffalo show highlighted by an epic Franklin’s Suite and a soaring Music Never Stopped jam.
review: The recording is a pristine soundboard dump that many reviewers call gorgeous, with only a brief reel‑change skip in the middle of The Music Never Stopped. Musically the concert is hailed as one of the band’s finest of ’77 – the first three tunes, the Help > Slipknot! > Franklin’s suite, Peggy‑O and the second‑set highlights are described as chilling and unforgettable.

ai_rating.rationale: Uniformly glowing reviews (9×5★, 1×4★) praise both the performance and the soundboard quality; only a minor technical skip prevents a perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix praised as superior to audience tapes. A reel‑change causes a short jump in the middle of The Music Never Stopped jam, but most listeners find it seamless.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as solid and favorable; after the break the band launches into a spectacular second set with seamless segues, delivering several emotional peaks.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar is called "crying" and full of energy; vocals praised alongside Bob's harmonies.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as "on point" during the performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for staying right behind Jerry, providing strong rhythm/vocals.
